reason: Gate F not run: no lead-time forecast experiment has been executed or validated.
first_experiment: 2021/22 + 2023/24 retrospective seasonal rainfall/yield at 1-3 month lead, southern Brazil / lower La Plata; score ALL available events per-event with a year-block interval (never read skill off the single most predictable La Nina).

leakage_exclusions: no target-season rainfall, yield, price, or outcome data in the predictors
leave-one-season-out; treat 2015/16 as ONE compound event
init date and information set logged for every issued forecast

baselines.name: climatology
baselines.role: skill-score reference; the floor to beat

baselines.name: concurrent_structural_engine
baselines.role: the static engine fed concurrent perfect-foresight rainfall; isolates the value the LEAD TIME adds over simply knowing the rain

uncertainty.honesty: five events can DEMONSTRATE skill but cannot CERTIFY calibrated performance; report per-event scores and a year-block interval over ~5 events for EVERY metric; never a calibrated-coverage claim off the small event set.

pass_criteria: leakage guard green (no season-Y data in the fit/predictor for season Y)
beats persistence AND climatology AND the concurrent structural engine
anomaly correlation up AND CRPSS > 0 AND BSS > 0, drought-detection ROC-AUC above the concurrent engine
every metric carries a year-block bootstrap interval over ~5 events with a non-negative lower bound
verdict logged via the provenance ledger with the string 'skill demonstrated on about five events, not operational or calibrated'
contract.halt_rule: HALT if any criterion fails. A failing gate is an honest go/no-go on TIMING, never a number to massage by widening intervals, dropping events, or pooling state-years.

_note: Honest scaffold, not a forecast model. Fetches no seasonal-forecast data and emits no forecast-conditioned loss number. A forecast claim is impossible until this gate PASSES.
